description: Python<->Elixir IPC layer. Provides (1) an aiohttp-backed Phoenix Channels v2 client with multi-topic multiplexing + auto-reconnect + bounded pending-push queue, (2) a typed envelope schema with builders for event/directive/directive_ack/handler_call/handler_reply (wire invariant: source must be an esr:// URI), (3) adapter_runner and handler_worker subprocess entry points (python -m esr.ipc.adapter_runner / handler_worker) that join topic `adapter:<name>/<instance>` or `handler:<module>/<worker_id>` and dispatch envelopes FIFO, (4) URL discovery that distinguishes the /adapter_hub and /handler_hub Phoenix sockets.

common_fields.kind: duplicate of `type` (legacy; set by builders, e.g. channel_client.py:214 dispatches on it)
common_fields.id: string; event=`e-<uuid4>`, directive=`d-<uuid4>`, handler_call=`h-<uuid4>`. For *_ack/*_reply the id is the ORIGINAL message id (correlation) -- envelope.py:8-10, 89-91, 115-116
common_fields.source: REQUIRED `esr://...` URI; enforced by _check_source (envelope.py:50-53) -- wire invariant per spec 7.5

payloads.directive: {action: str, args: dict} -- consumed by process_directive adapter_runner.py:43-64
payloads.directive_ack: {ok: bool, result?: dict, error?: {type, message}} -- make_directive_ack envelope.py:77-104
payloads.handler_call: {handler: str, state: dict (with _schema_version), event: {source, event_type, args}} -- process_handler_call handler_worker.py:35-100
payloads.handler_reply: {new_state: dict (stamped with _schema_version), actions: [serialised Action dict]} | {error: {type, message}} -- make_handler_reply envelope.py:107-129

phoenix_channel_client.wire_format: Phoenix v2 array frame `[join_ref, ref, topic, event, payload]` (channel_client.py:193-197; parsed at 209)

methods.join(topic, on_msg): sends phx_join, awaits phx_reply status=ok; raises RuntimeError if rejected (channel_client.py:120-144)
methods.push(topic, event, payload): sends on joined topic; queues into bounded deque (PENDING_PUSH_CAP=1000) while disconnected, dropping oldest on overflow (channel_client.py:146-165)
methods.close(): cancels tasks, closes WS/session, disables reconnect (channel_client.py:101-116)

phoenix_channel_client.reconnect: on auto_reconnect=True: exponential backoff (1,2,4,8,16,30s) -- channel_client.py:52, 237-293; re-joins topics with fresh refs and flushes pending deque in FIFO order

channel_pusher.role: adapts ChannelClient.push to the AdapterPusher protocol (`source_uri: str` + `async push_envelope(env)`) used by adapter_runner.directive_loop/event_loop; always pushes with phoenix event name `envelope` (channel_pusher.py:42)

adapter_runner.process_directive(adapter, payload): calls adapter.on_directive(action, args); wraps success/exc (adapter_runner.py:43-64). KeyError on missing action propagates.
adapter_runner.directive_loop(adapter, queue, pusher): drains queue FIFO; None=sentinel; pushes directive_ack via make_directive_ack reusing envelope['id'] (adapter_runner.py:67-89)
adapter_runner.event_loop(adapter, pusher): iterates adapter.emit_events() async gen, wraps each {event_type, args} as event envelope (adapter_runner.py:92-108)
adapter_runner.run_with_client(adapter, client, topic): connects, joins topic with _on_frame that filters envelopes with kind='directive' onto a queue; runs directive_loop + event_loop in TaskGroup (adapter_runner.py:111-152). source_uri derived as `esr://localhost/<topic>` (line 145).
adapter_runner.run(adapter_name, instance_id, config, u: loads factory via esr.adapters.load_adapter_factory, wraps config in AdapterConfig, joins topic `adapter:<name>/<instance>` (adapter_runner.py:155-180)

handler_worker.process_handler_call(payload): pure dispatcher -- resolves HANDLER_REGISTRY[payload.handler], validates STATE_REGISTRY[actor_type], enforces _schema_version match (returns SchemaVersionMismatch error on drift), reconstructs pydantic state and Event, invokes handler_entry.fn(state, event), serialises actions; NEVER raises -- all failures become {error:{type,message}} (handler_worker.py:35-100)
handler_worker._dump_state: serialises pydantic model and coerces frozenset/set/tuple to JSON-native lists (handler_worker.py:107-134)
handler_worker.run_with_client(client, topic): connects, joins topic with _on_frame filtering kind='handler_call' onto queue; for each call, pushes handler_reply envelope `{kind:handler_reply, id:<call_id>, source:esr://localhost/<topic>, payload:reply_payload}` back on same topic (handler_worker.py:137-174)
handler_worker.run(handler_module, worker_id, url): importlib.import_module(`esr_handler_<pkg>.on_msg`) then falls back to raw dotted module; joins topic `handler:<module>/<worker_id>` (handler_worker.py:177-198)

url_discovery.discover_runtime_url(override, kind='ada: priority: override > ESR_{ADAPTER|HANDLER}_HUB_URL > ESR_RUNTIME_URL (legacy adapter alias) > DEFAULT_{ADAPTER|HANDLER}_HUB_URL (url.py:42-80)

topic_naming.phoenix_event_name: All envelopes are pushed under Phoenix event name `envelope` (channel_pusher.py:42, handler_worker.py:167)

subprocess_launch.adapter_runner: Invoked as `uv run --project py python -m esr.ipc.adapter_runner --adapter <name> --instance-id <id> --url <adapter_hub_ws_url> --config-json '<json>'`. Args parsed by _parse_main_args (adapter_runner.py:183-201). Config-JSON loaded via json.loads (line 200). stdin is unused. Env inherits ESR_ADAPTER_HUB_URL/ESR_RUNTIME_URL but CLI --url overrides. asyncio.run(run(...)) runs indefinitely until SIGINT/cancellation; KeyboardInterrupt -> exit 0, any other BaseException -> exit 1 with traceback to stderr (lines 215-226). Test evidence: test_adapter_runner_main.py:25-79.
subprocess_launch.handler_worker: Invoked as `python -m esr.ipc.handler_worker --module <dotted> --worker-id <id> --url <handler_hub_ws_url>`. Args parsed by _parse_main_args (handler_worker.py:201-212). On startup imports `esr_handler_<pkg>.on_msg` to trigger @handler decorator registration (lines 190-195). stdin is unused. Test evidence: test_handler_worker_main.py:16-55.

external: pydantic (handler state models; handler_worker.py and tests)
elixir_counterparts: runtime/lib/esr/ipc/envelope.ex (PRD 01 F09-F12; constants kept in lock-step per envelope.py:16-17)

test_results.command: cd py && uv run pytest tests/test_adapter_loader.py tests/test_adapter_manifest.py tests/test_adapter_runner.py tests/test_adapter_runner_main.py tests/test_adapter_runner_run.py tests/test_channel_client.py tests/test_channel_client_call.py tests/test_channel_pusher.py tests/test_envelope.py tests/test_handler_worker.py tests/test_handler_worker_main.py tests/test_handler_worker_run.py tests/test_ipc_integration.py tests/test_url_discovery.py -q
test_results.notes: 1 skip is test_ipc_integration.py live-runtime round-trip gated behind ESR_E2E_RUNTIME=1 (test_ipc_integration.py:31-34).

user_flows.name: adapter directive round-trip (runtime -> adapter)
steps: Elixir runtime pushes envelope {kind:directive, id:d-..., source:esr://..., payload:{action,args}} on topic adapter:<name>/<instance>
directive_loop dequeues FIFO, calls adapter.on_directive(action,args), builds make_directive_ack(id_=original) (lines 77-89)

user_flows.name: adapter event emission (adapter -> runtime)
steps: Adapter implements async generator emit_events() yielding {event_type, args}
event_loop wraps each in make_event envelope and pushes on topic (adapter_runner.py:92-108)

user_flows.name: handler call round-trip (runtime -> handler worker)
steps: HandlerRouter pushes envelope {kind:handler_call, id:h-..., payload:{handler, state, event}} on handler:<module>/<worker>
process_handler_call resolves registries, checks _schema_version, invokes handler_entry.fn, serialises actions (lines 35-100)
run_with_client pushes handler_reply envelope on same topic with original id (lines 160-172)

user_flows.name: disconnect/reconnect with pending push preservation
steps: push() during window appends to bounded deque PENDING_PUSH_CAP=1000, drops oldest on overflow (lines 156-163)
_reconnect_loop retries with backoff 1/2/4/8/16/30s, re-joins topics with fresh refs, flushes pending pushes FIFO (lines 237-293)

notes: Envelope has both `kind` and `type` set to the same discriminator -- the `kind` field is what _on_frame dispatches on (adapter_runner.py:136, handler_worker.py:154); presumably legacy/redundant but tests assert both.
The Phoenix event name for all application envelopes is the literal string `envelope`; phx_reply / phx_join are reserved for protocol frames (channel_client.py:214, 137).
Reviewer C1 fix: split adapter_hub vs handler_hub URLs; legacy ESR_RUNTIME_URL remains an adapter-only alias (url.py documentation + test_url_discovery.py).

Reviewer S7 fix: _read_loop wraps frame JSON decode + arity unpack in try/except so one bad frame does not tear down the socket (channel_client.py:207-212; covered by test_channel_client_tolerates_malformed_frames).
Reviewer S3 fix: process_handler_call catches KeyError/TypeError/AttributeError on envelope access and surfaces MalformedEnvelope instead of raising (handler_worker.py:43-51).
PRD 02 F05 / Reviewer C2 fix: state dicts carry `_schema_version`; mismatch returns SchemaVersionMismatch error; replies stamp the registered version on new_state (handler_worker.py:64-95).
Frozenset/set values in pydantic state are coerced to sorted lists before JSON serialisation (handler_worker.py:124-134) -- Phoenix JSON encoder does not accept sets.
adapter_runner.run imports esr.adapters at runtime to avoid circular imports and to defer optional adapter loading.
handler_worker.run module-import heuristic: tries `esr_handler_<first_segment>.on_msg` first, falls back to raw dotted path for test shims (handler_worker.py:190-195).
The live-runtime integration test (test_ipc_integration.py) is the only skipped test; gated on ESR_E2E_RUNTIME=1 with a running Phoenix server on ws://localhost:4001.
